name: Potential Unix Shell Command and Scripting Interpreter
notes: This rule triggers when suspicious shell commands or program code that may be executed for command and scripting interpreters.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Activity in Shell Commands
BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Reverse Shell Command Line
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Potential Equation Group Indicators, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Possible Linux Privilege Escalation, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Reverse Shell Command Line, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Activity in Shell Commands, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Commands Linux, BB:BehaviorDefinition: BPFtrace Unsafe Option Usage
TD_list: APPLY Potential Unix Shell Command and Scripting Interpreter on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Potential Equation Group Indicators, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Possible Linux Privilege Escalation, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Reverse Shell Command Line, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Activity in Shell Commands, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Commands Linux, BB:BehaviorDefinition: BPFtrace Unsafe Option Usage
RED_list: Event name: Potential Unix Shell Command and Scripting Interpreter
Event description: Detects when suspicious shell commands or program code that may be executed for command and scripting interpreters.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
